title: pisum arvense subsp. biflorum
intro: Pisum arvense subsp. biflorum, also known as wild pea or field pea, is a subspecies of the Pisum arvense plant, a type of flowering plant in the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Pisum arvense subsp. biflorum include its potential to help manage digestive problems, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce stress. Herbal preparations of Pisum arvense subsp. biflorum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
